title: When meeting a girl that's potentially worth it, ask what book she's reading (Aug 28 2022 dating heuristic)
html:
TL;DR

  • One-line dating heuristic captured Aug 28, 2022 (5:55am, end of the Vietnam summer): "When meeting a girl that potentially worth ir, ask , what book are you currently reading."
  • A Yahir-canonical filter: instead of asking what someone does or likes, ask what they're currently reading — a present-tense, low-prep, high-signal question. Cousin to the asking the right question / piercing prompts genre but applied to romantic vetting.
  • Captured one week before the "I have never really been in love" note — Yahir was building dating-frameworks while simultaneously logging that he had no live-fire experience to test them on.
  • Slots into the small dating-heuristics corpus alongside the Emily arc notes years later (2025) — the "what book are you reading" question almost certainly recurs there.
  • The "potentially worth ir" typo (= "worth it") is preserved in the original — a marker of the early-morning capture state.

Key quote

"When meeting a girl that potentially worth ir, ask , what book are you currently reading"

Why it matters

This is one of the cleanest specimens of Yahir applying the asking the right question frame to a non-business domain. The same instinct that makes him collect Munger maxims and catalog piercing prompts for AI also generates a one-line vetting question for dating. The question works because the answer to "what are you reading" is behaviorally observable (you either have a book in flight or you don't) and taste-revealing (the genre, the choice, the seriousness all encode signal).

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually ask this question in the field, and if so to whom?
  • Does the Emily arc in 2025 contain a "what are you reading" exchange?
  • Is there a parallel heuristic for other relationships (a "what are you reading" for friends, mentors, hires)?
